title: MeshCore One - An open source MeshCore client for iOS/iPadOS

messages.author: avion
messages.text: Announcing the beta of MeshCore One (MC1), an open source MeshCore client written in Swift. Notable features: Line of Sight tool that allows adding a repeater in the path to see if adding one would make the path viable Trace Path that allows saving trace paths, batching trace paths, and seeing statistics Link previews Reactions Inline gif/image previews Block/favorite contacts Mute/favorite chats Battery OCV curve presets and custom tuning WiFi node support CLI Full iPad support with split views Future features: Room management Guest repeater management view macOS support Import/Export config settings Translations What doesn't work: You tell me! (where my xda oldies at) iOS/iPadOS 18 compatibility may be rough. I don’t have a physical device in 18, so there are probably bugs I haven’t caught in the simulator. It's possible the app has bugs in busy networks. I don't have many nodes/activity happening where I am so couldn't stress test reliably. Let me know if you run into any bugs or have feature ideas, I'd love to hear them GitHub repo: https://github.com/Avi0n/MeshCoreOne TestFlight link: https://testflight.apple.com/join/YjGNDTaE OS requirements: iOS >= 18.0 iPadOS >= 18.0 Disclaimer: This app was mostly built with AI and I am not a professional developer. (edited)
messages.content_html:
Announcing the beta of MeshCore One (MC1), an open source MeshCore client written in Swift. Notable features:
  • Line of Sight tool that allows adding a repeater in the path to see if adding one would make the path viable
  • Trace Path that allows saving trace paths, batching trace paths, and seeing statistics
  • Link previews
  • Reactions
  • Inline gif/image previews
  • Block/favorite contacts
  • Mute/favorite chats
  • Battery OCV curve presets and custom tuning
  • WiFi node support
  • CLI
  • Full iPad support with split views Future features:
  • Room management
  • Guest repeater management view
  • macOS support
  • Import/Export config settings
  • Translations What doesn't work:
  • You tell me! (where my xda oldies at)
  • iOS/iPadOS 18 compatibility may be rough. I don’t have a physical device in 18, so there are probably bugs I haven’t caught in the simulator.
  • It's possible the app has bugs in busy networks. I don't have many nodes/activity happening where I am so couldn't stress test reliably. Let me know if you run into any bugs or have feature ideas, I'd love to hear them GitHub repo: https://github.com/Avi0n/MeshCoreOne
TestFlight link: https://testflight.apple.com/join/YjGNDTaE OS requirements:
  • iOS >= 18.0
  • iPadOS >= 18.0 Disclaimer: This app was mostly built with AI and I am not a professional developer.
 (edited)

messages.author: avion
messages.text: Thanks for testing! So far, nothing really sets it apart except for the Line of Sight tool. And that is by design. My goal with this app is to create an Apple native experience version of the official MeshCore app. No fancy additions unless people really want them. Extensions to features such as the line of sight tool are good too. And hopefully PocketMesh has more reliable bluetooth connections than the official MeshCore iOS app.

messages.author: avion
messages.text: Version 0.4.0 has been submitted for review! Release notes below. What's New Revamped Contacts Tab : Now rebranded as "Nodes", featuring category switching between Contacts and Network devices (repeaters & rooms). Also added sorting e.g. last heard advert. Updated Map : Visualize clusters with numbered indicators and added map layers. Batch Trace Mode Introduced : Trace Path tool has a new batch processing mode, including averaging for better trace insights. Refined SNR Attribution : Transitioned from sender to receiver attribution in Trace Path SNR values. Unread Mentions : A new button in chats scrolls you to your unread mentions. Mute Conversations Easily : Swipe any conversation to mute it. Block Contacts : Swipe a Contact in the Node list to block unwanted interactions. This will delete all chats with the Contact and prevent their messages from showing in channels. Mention Picker : Type "@" to quickly select contacts to mention. Hashtag Channel Integration : Tap hashtag channels in messages to join or create them on the fly. More Accurate Battery Percentage : Automatically set OCV battery curve based on device manufacturer during first pair. Enhanced Pill Status Indicators : Added more pill statuses including Connecting, Connected, and Disconnected. Quick Device Connection : Tap the pill in Disconnected state to open the Connect Device sheet from anywhere in the app. Intelligent Connection Detection : Detect and prevent connections if BLE device is connected to another app. (edited)
messages.content_html:
Version 0.4.0 has been submitted for review! Release notes below. What's New
  • Revamped Contacts Tab: Now rebranded as "Nodes", featuring category switching between Contacts and Network devices (repeaters & rooms). Also added sorting e.g. last heard advert.
  • Updated Map: Visualize clusters with numbered indicators and added map layers.
  • Batch Trace Mode Introduced: Trace Path tool has a new batch processing mode, including averaging for better trace insights.
  • Refined SNR Attribution: Transitioned from sender to receiver attribution in Trace Path SNR values.
  • Unread Mentions: A new button in chats scrolls you to your unread mentions.
  • Mute Conversations Easily: Swipe any conversation to mute it.
  • Block Contacts: Swipe a Contact in the Node list to block unwanted interactions. This will delete all chats with the Contact and prevent their messages from showing in channels.
  • Mention Picker: Type "@" to quickly select contacts to mention.
  • Hashtag Channel Integration: Tap hashtag channels in messages to join or create them on the fly.
  • More Accurate Battery Percentage: Automatically set OCV battery curve based on device manufacturer during first pair.
  • Enhanced Pill Status Indicators: Added more pill statuses including Connecting, Connected, and Disconnected.
  • Quick Device Connection: Tap the pill in Disconnected state to open the Connect Device sheet from anywhere in the app.
  • Intelligent Connection Detection: Detect and prevent connections if BLE device is connected to another app.
 (edited)

What Changed Chat: Switch color to blue for all outgoing message bubbles Chat: Add ability to copy path in the Repeat Details section Map: See the Last Advert in node details Radio: Add/adjust Australia radio presets Sync: Now only does a partial contact sync while in background, no channel sync. Channel sync will occur upon opening the app. Trace Path: After loading a saved path, automatically scroll down to the Run Trace button Bug Fixes Fix choppy scrolling in chats when many messages were received Fix reconnect sync hang Fix sync failure when app was backgrounded Fix crash when attempting to type a password for a repeater on the Map tab Fix non-standard firmware version formats not displaying in repeater settings Fix Jump to Path button not disappearing on iPad Fix inability to save a path that was previously saved but deleted (edited)
messages.content_html:
PocketMesh 0.4.1 has been submitted to Apple for review There are 3 things to really pay attention to and test for this version: 1. All things syncing. messages.author: avion
messages.text: 0.4.2 has been submitted to Apple for review! In addition to continuing to keep an eye out for Syncing bugs (especially Contacts) and chat view UI glitches from 0.4.1, keep an eye out for stuck pill statuses, pills that should be there but aren't, etc. In this release, I refactored the Status Pill's code to be simpler and easier to maintain. But this also means I could have missed some edge cases again. Thank you in advance! What Changed Chats: Swipe a DM/Channel/Room to the right to Favorite Status Pill: Now has animations instead of just appearing/disappearing instantly Status Pill: Removed Channels and Contacts from the pill status as it felt too busy for an Apple app New "Send Again" button in long press menu for messages that failed to send or had 0 heard repeats Added logging for radio settings (Frequency, Spreading Factor, etc.) Bug Fixes Fixed a bug where swiping to delete a contact would cause a crash Fixed a bug where occasionally tapping on a node that was clustered would automatically zoom you out Fixed crashes during transitional states (closing sheets, backing out, opening the app launcher) iPad now reflects the correct radio settings if you have Advanced Settings open and are looking at the Radio section in the main Settings panel at the same time Fix rooms not updating badge notifications reliably Fix room notifications not clearing after reading Fix rooms not sorting by last received time Fix room message bubble text being black instead of white As always, thank you for all the TestFlight feedback reports and GitHub issues. I really appreciate them (edited)
messages.content_html:
0.4.2 has been submitted to Apple for review! In addition to continuing to keep an eye out for Syncing bugs (especially Contacts) and chat view UI glitches from 0.4.1, keep an eye out for stuck pill statuses, pills that should be there but aren't, etc. In this release, I refactored the Status Pill's code to be simpler and easier to maintain. But this also means I could have missed some edge cases again. Thank you in advance! What Changed
  • Chats: Swipe a DM/Channel/Room to the right to Favorite
  • Status Pill: Now has animations instead of just appearing/disappearing instantly
  • Status Pill: Removed Channels and Contacts from the pill status as it felt too busy for an Apple app
  • New "Send Again" button in long press menu for messages that failed to send or had 0 heard repeats
  • Added logging for radio settings (Frequency, Spreading Factor, etc.)
Bug Fixes
  • Fixed a bug where swiping to delete a contact would cause a crash
  • Fixed a bug where occasionally tapping on a node that was clustered would automatically zoom you out
  • Fixed crashes during transitional states (closing sheets, backing out, opening the app launcher)
  • iPad now reflects the correct radio settings if you have Advanced Settings open and are looking at the Radio section in the main Settings panel at the same time
  • Fix rooms not updating badge notifications reliably
  • Fix room notifications not clearing after reading
  • Fix rooms not sorting by last received time
  • Fix room message bubble text being black instead of white
As always, thank you for all the TestFlight feedback reports and GitHub issues. I really appreciate them (edited)

messages.author: avion
messages.text: PocketMesh 0.5.0 has been submitted to TestFlight for review! What's Changed Multilingual Support : Added localization for 7 new languages: Dutch, French, German, Polish, Russian, Spanish, and Ukrainian. These were translated by AI, so expect some many quirks. Want to fix them or add more? Check out TRANSLATIONS.md on GitHub. PRs are super welcome! Noise Floor Monitoring Tool : Introduced a new tool to monitor noise floor with handy statistics. Total Distance in Trace Path : Now view the total distance for successful traces in the Trace Path tool. Repeater Input Field : Added a new field in Trace Path to easily type or paste repeaters. Repeater Favorites : Added a new toggle in Trace Path to only show your favorite repeaters. Map Mode in Trace Path : Check out the new Map mode in the Trace Path tool! Message Path Viewer : Long press on a received message to see its incoming path (requires your node to be connected to PM at the time). Pinned Favorite Chats : Favorites are now pinned right at the top of your chat list. Reply From Channel Notifications : Added "Reply" to notification banners so you can reply without opening the app. Works on Apple Watch notifications too! Enhanced Sender Names : In Channels and Rooms, sender names are now bigger, bolder, and more colorful. Grouped Messages : Subsequent messages from the same sender are now neatly grouped together. Expanded Mention Picker : Now includes recent channel senders for quicker mentions. Refreshed Color Palette : Updated the overall color palette. Flexible Repeater Login : Allow empty password login for ACL authentication. Auto-Zoom Location Setting : When setting node or repeater location, it auto-zooms to your GPS spot if location permissions are enabled. Smart Button Disabling : Buttons across the app are disabled when the radio is disconnected. Offline Data Browsing : Browse data from previously connected devices even when disconnected. Haptic Feedback on Copies : Added haptic feedback to all copy/paste buttons for better feel. Polished UI/UX : A little smoother animation here, a little less jank there. ## Bug Fixes Fixed crash by validating lat/lon for contacts on the map. Fixed crash by clamping initial camera zoom-out on the map. Fixed rooms not sorting by last message received date in chats. Fixed rooms lingering in the chats list after node deletion. Fixed ability to create duplicate hashtag channels in chats. Fixed contacts incorrectly matching in the "Repeat Details" sheet in chats. Attempted fix for location not saving in settings, with added logging for troubleshooting. Fixed inability to clear a location from the node in settings. Fixed some repeaters not showing their name in Trace Results in tools. Fixed chat input bar ignoring floating keyboard on iPad. Fixed nodes not deleting from the database if removed in another app during sync. Fixed incomplete contact sync by performing a full sync on first or manual connection. I'm not super happy with the input field UX in Trace Path. But it works and is important for busy networks so I wanted to get it out ASAP even with its quirks. I've been getting lots of great feedback, crash reports, and feature requests. Thank you! I appreciate each and every one of them (edited)
messages.content_html:
PocketMesh 0.5.0 has been submitted to TestFlight for review!

What's Changed

  • Multilingual Support: Added localization for 7 new languages: Dutch, French, German, Polish, Russian, Spanish, and Ukrainian. These were translated by AI, so expect some many quirks. Want to fix them or add more? Check out TRANSLATIONS.md on GitHub. PRs are super welcome!
  • Noise Floor Monitoring Tool: Introduced a new tool to monitor noise floor with handy statistics.
  • Total Distance in Trace Path: Now view the total distance for successful traces in the Trace Path tool.
  • Repeater Input Field: Added a new field in Trace Path to easily type or paste repeaters.
  • Repeater Favorites: Added a new toggle in Trace Path to only show your favorite repeaters.
  • Map Mode in Trace Path: Check out the new Map mode in the Trace Path tool!
  • Message Path Viewer: Long press on a received message to see its incoming path (requires your node to be connected to PM at the time).
  • Pinned Favorite Chats: Favorites are now pinned right at the top of your chat list.
  • Reply From Channel Notifications: Added "Reply" to notification banners so you can reply without opening the app. Works on Apple Watch notifications too!
  • Enhanced Sender Names: In Channels and Rooms, sender names are now bigger, bolder, and more colorful.
  • Grouped Messages: Subsequent messages from the same sender are now neatly grouped together.
  • Expanded Mention Picker: Now includes recent channel senders for quicker mentions.
  • Refreshed Color Palette: Updated the overall color palette.
  • Flexible Repeater Login: Allow empty password login for ACL authentication.
  • Auto-Zoom Location Setting: When setting node or repeater location, it auto-zooms to your GPS spot if location permissions are enabled.
  • Smart Button Disabling: Buttons across the app are disabled when the radio is disconnected.
  • Offline Data Browsing: Browse data from previously connected devices even when disconnected.
  • Haptic Feedback on Copies: Added haptic feedback to all copy/paste buttons for better feel.
  • Polished UI/UX: A little smoother animation here, a little less jank there. ## Bug Fixes
  • Fixed crash by validating lat/lon for contacts on the map.
  • Fixed crash by clamping initial camera zoom-out on the map.
  • Fixed rooms not sorting by last message received date in chats.
  • Fixed rooms lingering in the chats list after node deletion.
  • Fixed ability to create duplicate hashtag channels in chats.
  • Fixed contacts incorrectly matching in the "Repeat Details" sheet in chats.
  • Attempted fix for location not saving in settings, with added logging for troubleshooting.
  • Fixed inability to clear a location from the node in settings.
  • Fixed some repeaters not showing their name in Trace Results in tools.
  • Fixed chat input bar ignoring floating keyboard on iPad.
  • Fixed nodes not deleting from the database if removed in another app during sync.
  • Fixed incomplete contact sync by performing a full sync on first or manual connection. I'm not super happy with the input field UX in Trace Path. But it works and is important for busy networks so I wanted to get it out ASAP even with its quirks. I've been getting lots of great feedback, crash reports, and feature requests. Thank you! I appreciate each and every one of them
(edited)

messages.text: hm... I'm hesitant to add anything that will require certain apps to see/interact with. But if more people ask for it, I may consider adding it under advanced options. The other thing for me to consider is rate limits. I think meshcore open uses giphy, and the free API key is rate limited to 100 searches/API calls an hour. Which PocketMesh probably won't hit anytime soon with its little over 100 users, but if it eventually does, I'd have to start paying out of pocket for it. It also is not good practice to include the API key in the source code, so I'd have to spin up a small hetzner server or something similar to act as a proxy where the actual API key lives. Which would cost about $5/mo. (edited)

messages.author: avion
messages.text: Hey thanks for testing PocketMesh! Really happy to hear you're enjoying it so far I'll answer your questions below. The message header will tell you whether the path is Direct or Flood. As long as the route is not Flood, it is considered Direct. If it is 0 hop, you will see "Direct 0 hop". Yes, the auto reset path is on by default. If the contact had a direct path but the path no longer works, it will switch to Flood after 3 attempts. Then it will try Flood twice. You should be able to see the retry status underneath the message and in the header underneath the contact's name. Once all 5 tries are exhausted, the message will turn red and let you know it failed, with an option to Retry sending. If this isn't the behavior you're seeing, it's a bug and I'd like to fix it. What version are you on and which OS? By "already used", do you mean you already had it paired to your phone using Liam's app? I had only tested the auto battery curve with a fresh pair to PocketMesh, so it's possible it doesn't set the curve if the device was already paired in iOS/iPadOS. I will look into this. Thank you so much for the feedback. Let me know if you run into any other bugs, if anything else seems confusing, or have any feature requests!
messages.content_html:

  • Yes, the auto reset path is on by default. If the contact had a direct path but the path no longer works, it will switch to Flood after 3 attempts. Then it will try Flood twice. You should be able to see the retry status underneath the message and in the header underneath the contact's name. Once all 5 tries are exhausted, the message will turn red and let you know it failed, with an option to Retry sending. If this isn't the behavior you're seeing, it's a bug and I'd like to fix it. What version are you on and which OS?
  • By "already used", do you mean you already had it paired to your phone using Liam's app? I had only tested the auto battery curve with a fresh pair to PocketMesh, so it's possible it doesn't set the curve if the device was already paired in iOS/iPadOS. I will look into this. Thank you so much for the feedback. Let me know if you run into any other bugs, if anything else seems confusing, or have any feature requests!

messages.content_html:
0.6.0 has been submitted for review! Sorry for the long wait on this one.

What's Changed

Chat
  • Infinite scroll pagination for loading older messages
  • Improved message retry feedback showing attempt count
Discovery & Contacts
  • Node count displayed in search placeholder
  • Filtering, search, sorting, and clear functionality in the Discover page
  • Show all discovered nodes on discover page
v1.12 Firmware Support
  • Auto-add settings and node storage UI
  • Contact deletion protocol support
  • Backward compatibility for pre-v1.12 firmware
Advanced Settings
  • Updated Node settings to include toggles for firmware v1.12 features
Notifications
  • Tapping channel notifications now navigates directly to that channel
CLI
  • New CLI tool for repeater administration
  • ZSH style text suggestions and tab completion
  • Sessions allow you to manage multiple repeaters/rooms at once
Localization
  • Added some missing UI translations
  • Improved date formatting using Apple's localized formats
Other Improvements
  • Improved BLE state restoration handling
  • Better path display truncation for long hop lists in RX log

Bug Fixes

  • BLE: Resolved contact sync failures and race conditions (packet ordering, write pacing, continuation leaks)
  • Badge counts: Now correctly filtered by current device only (multi-device users saw wrong counts)
  • Messages: URLs within @mentions no longer incorrectly parsed as links or show link previews
  • Added repeater first byte collision handling. Helps with any screen that shows a list of repeaters. Selects repeaters in this order: Distance (if location services enabled) > Last advert time IPA is available for those who sideload: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.6.0
 (edited)

messages.text: I don’t think so, but I will definitely double check. When I researched it, it seemed BLE was the only thing Apple allows to be semi-persistent in the background. The only way to wake up an app in the background is to send a push notification via Apple Push Notifications. The way that would work is to have a node connected to a small program on a computer that acts as a proxy and sends/receives notifications to a central server which would then send out an APN to your app which would allow it to wake up. But that would require me to spin up a server and has a bunch of privacy implications. (edited)

messages.text: 0.7.0 has been submitted for review! What's Changed Notification Settings New granular channel notification settings: All, Mentions Only, Mute. Access by tapping the "i" in the upper right of a channel. Not yet available in DMs. Reactions Send and receive reactions In order to match the reactions to the correct message, a hash is created and sent along with the reaction. Example: @[user] b45pc4ek This was done in an effort to be as friendly to other clients as possible. Note: Reactions will only be rendered as a proper reaction if the other user is using PocketMesh or their client has implemented the same way PocketMesh is creating hashes. If you are a client developer and would like to add interoperability support, check out https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/blob/dev/docs/Reactions.md GPS Location Updates Added support for both device and phone GPS location updates Updates your node's location every time you send an advert Ping Repeater You can now ping repeaters if they are 0 hop. Access the new button by selecting a repeater in the Nodes tab. New Message Divider If you've received more than 10 new messages, you'll now see a "New Messages" divider CLI Add new nodes and channel commands Trace Path Map Optimizations No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map No more delay when tapping repeaters to build a path Repeaters don't disappear upon a successful trace, so you can add more repeaters after the trace Line of Sight Optimizations Added repeater labels and clustering No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map Added loading states so the app doesn't seem frozen if the API call to Open-Meteo is slow ## Bug Fixes Fix CLI text and toolbar buttons getting cut off when using Zoomed display and/or larger text Fix CLI not resetting state when disconnecting/changing devices Fix iPad not being able to start a DM with a contact Fix Telemetry toggles not persisting Fix Telemetry share with trusted contacts not functioning Fix Radio Configuration settings disappearing when clearing Frequency or TX Power Fix Map mode trace getting stuck if you had batch mode enabled in the List tab Workaround for issue on Line of Sight and Trace Path maps where tapping a repeater then immediately panning would trigger a zoom. This was done by completely disabling the double-tap-to-zoom feature. Sideload IPA is available here: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.7.0 Thank you for all the great bug reports and feedback (edited)
messages.content_html:
0.7.0 has been submitted for review!

What's Changed

Notification Settings
  • New granular channel notification settings: All, Mentions Only, Mute. Access by tapping the "i" in the upper right of a channel. Not yet available in DMs.
Reactions
  • Send and receive reactions
  • In order to match the reactions to the correct message, a hash is created and sent along with the reaction. Example:
@[user] b45pc4ek This was done in an effort to be as friendly to other clients as possible.
  • Note: Reactions will only be rendered as a proper reaction if the other user is using PocketMesh or their client has implemented the same way PocketMesh is creating hashes. If you are a client developer and would like to add interoperability support, check out https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/blob/dev/docs/Reactions.md GPS Location Updates
  • Added support for both device and phone GPS location updates
  • Updates your node's location every time you send an advert Ping Repeater
  • You can now ping repeaters if they are 0 hop. Access the new button by selecting a repeater in the Nodes tab. New Message Divider
  • If you've received more than 10 new messages, you'll now see a "New Messages" divider CLI
  • Add new nodes and channel commands Trace Path Map Optimizations
  • No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map
  • No more delay when tapping repeaters to build a path
  • Repeaters don't disappear upon a successful trace, so you can add more repeaters after the trace Line of Sight Optimizations
  • Added repeater labels and clustering
  • No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map
  • Added loading states so the app doesn't seem frozen if the API call to Open-Meteo is slow ## Bug Fixes
  • Fix CLI text and toolbar buttons getting cut off when using Zoomed display and/or larger text
  • Fix CLI not resetting state when disconnecting/changing devices
  • Fix iPad not being able to start a DM with a contact
  • Fix Telemetry toggles not persisting
  • Fix Telemetry share with trusted contacts not functioning
  • Fix Radio Configuration settings disappearing when clearing Frequency or TX Power
  • Fix Map mode trace getting stuck if you had batch mode enabled in the List tab
  • Workaround for issue on Line of Sight and Trace Path maps where tapping a repeater then immediately panning would trigger a zoom. This was done by completely disabling the double-tap-to-zoom feature. Sideload IPA is available here: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.7.0 Thank you for all the great bug reports and feedback
(edited)

messages.text: That isn’t possible yet, but will be soon. I’m adding a way to do that in the next release, along with the option to block key words. But it will be somewhat limited due to the way channels work in MeshCore. We can add a sender name to the block list, but since we don’t receive their key in channels, it will have to be matched by their sender name only which comes with two caveats: 1) The sender can easily change their sender name. 2) If another user has the same name they will also be blocked. That being said, I think adding the “Block Sender” feature’s benefits out weigh the downsides. (edited)

messages.content_html:
0.8.0 has been submitted to TF for approval!

What's Changed

  • Inline Images & GIFs: When you receive a link to a gif or image, see it in-line instead of having to tap the link. Also supports the meshcore-open gif format. But please note that I have no plans to include a gif picker to send gifs at this time.
  • Channel Sender Blocking: Block senders within channels by long pressing on their message. Manage blocked senders and contacts in Settings.
  • Telemetry Status History: See a history of your repeater's telemetry. (Only saves new data when loaded at least 15 minutes apart.)
  • Repeater Receive Errors: Support for showing packet receive errors in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Telemetry Grouping: Channel grouping in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Remove Non-Favorite Nodes: New Remove non-favorite nodes button found in Settings > Advanced Settings.
  • Companion Config Export/Import: Export and import node configuration. Found in Settings > Advanced Settings. Compatible with Liam's MeshCore app.
  • Companion Repeat Mode: Support for the new Repeat Mode. Your companion must be on firmware >= v1.13.0
  • Bluetooth Signal Strength: See bluetooth signal strength to your saved nodes in the Connect sheet.
  • Chinese Translation Corrections: Thank you @WZNRXSN ## Bug Fixes
  • Fix inability to enter periods in WiFi node connection sheets for locales that use comma instead of period.
  • Fix device not being removed from system Bluetooth when factory resetting companion device
  • Fix iPad gap between keyboard and input field

messages.author: avion
messages.text: 0.9.0 has been submitted for review! It usually takes about a business day for the review to complete. What's Changed Node Discovery: New tool to scan for nearby repeaters and sensors. Find it in Tools. Jump to Unread: New floating button in channels that scrolls you back to where you left off. Settings redesigned: Reorganized into sub-pages (Radio, Location, Connection, Chat) for easier navigation. Stale Node Cleanup: Automatically removes nodes older than a configurable time period after connecting to your radio. Toggle and configure in Settings > Advanced. Device Reboot: Reboot your radio directly from Settings > Advanced. Telemetry charts: Battery/voltage graphs now use the repeater's Battery Curve to set the Y-axis range, with a 500 mV buffer so charging at 100% stays visible. Chat reply format: Quoted text is now more visually distinct. A proper reply bubble is planned. Message actions sheet: Removed Liquid Glass transparency for better readability. Path hops are more compact so you can see more at a glance. Chat UI: Tighter spacing between messages, wider bubbles, mentions styled with underline instead of bold. RX Log: Entries now show the date alongside the timestamp. BLE device support: Added default name prefixes for nRF52 and WisCore boards. ### Bug Fixes Fixed keyboard gap on iPadOS 18. Enlarged the message send button on iOS/iPadOS 18. Tools on iPad now close when the radio disconnects to prevent stale data. Added missing localizations for Low Battery notifications. Fixed Settings buttons on iPad not appearing blue. Fixed "Connected elsewhere" showing incorrectly when the radio was not connected elsewhere. Fixed repeater name sometimes not populating in the Repeater Admin sheet. Chinese translation fixes (thanks MGJ520). Unsigned sideload IPAs available at https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.9.0
messages.content_html:
0.9.0 has been submitted for review! It usually takes about a business day for the review to complete.

What's Changed

  • Node Discovery: New tool to scan for nearby repeaters and sensors. Find it in Tools.
  • Jump to Unread: New floating button in channels that scrolls you back to where you left off.
  • Settings redesigned: Reorganized into sub-pages (Radio, Location, Connection, Chat) for easier navigation.
  • Stale Node Cleanup: Automatically removes nodes older than a configurable time period after connecting to your radio. Toggle and configure in Settings > Advanced.
  • Device Reboot: Reboot your radio directly from Settings > Advanced.
  • Telemetry charts: Battery/voltage graphs now use the repeater's Battery Curve to set the Y-axis range, with a 500 mV buffer so charging at 100% stays visible.
  • Chat reply format: Quoted text is now more visually distinct. A proper reply bubble is planned.
  • Message actions sheet: Removed Liquid Glass transparency for better readability. Path hops are more compact so you can see more at a glance.
  • Chat UI: Tighter spacing between messages, wider bubbles, mentions styled with underline instead of bold.
  • RX Log: Entries now show the date alongside the timestamp.
  • BLE device support: Added default name prefixes for nRF52 and WisCore boards. ### Bug Fixes
  • Fixed keyboard gap on iPadOS 18.
  • Enlarged the message send button on iOS/iPadOS 18.
  • Tools on iPad now close when the radio disconnects to prevent stale data.
  • Added missing localizations for Low Battery notifications.
  • Fixed Settings buttons on iPad not appearing blue.
  • Fixed "Connected elsewhere" showing incorrectly when the radio was not connected elsewhere.
  • Fixed repeater name sometimes not populating in the Repeater Admin sheet.
  • Chinese translation fixes (thanks MGJ520). Unsigned sideload IPAs available at https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.9.0

messages.content_html:
0.10.0 has been submitted for review! It takes about a full business day to pass review.

What's Changed

  • Connect Device: One less tap to connect—simply tap on your device. Long press nodes to edit or remove them.
  • Live Activity: View your node’s connection status, packets per minute, and battery level at a glance. Enabled by default (disable in Settings).
  • Settings: Added direct link to system Settings to change the app language.
  • Advanced Settings: Import a pre-generated private key or generate your own. Optionally generate keys with a specific prefix.
  • Chat: @mention suggestions now sorted by most recent sender instead of alphabetically.
  • Chat: Detect malicious links and warn the user
  • Chat: Send button now has a 1-second cooldown after sending a message.
  • Chat: On iPad, the sheet that opens when long pressing on a message is now taller by default
  • Chat List: Added persistent filter tabs at the top of the Chats list and removed the filter button.
  • Node Avatars: Now shows emoji if name contains one.
  • Repeater Admin: Added “Contact Info” section.
  • Message Paths: Path names in long-press menus now resolve discovered node names when no contact exists.
  • Trace Path Tool: New “include discovered” toggle to select from nearby discovered nodes.
  • Trace Path Tool: Rooms now appear in the trace path list.
  • Discover: Discovered node rows now show the node ID hash for easier identification.
  • Node Discovery: Add discovered repeaters right from the tool. No need to go to Discovery to add. ### Bug Fixes
  • Fixed text input not clearing occasionally after sending a message.
  • Fixed crash in LoS tool when nodes are too spread out.
  • Removed accidental ability to chat with Room nodes.
  • Fixed Radio Button visual glitch on iPadOS 26.
  • Fix rooms saying "Connected" when the radio was manually disconnected
  • Centered emoji row in message long press sheet
  • Fix T1000-E not matching to OCV preset

  • Node Discovery: Add discovered repeaters right from the tool. No need to go to Discovery to add. ### Bug Fixes
  • Fixed text input not clearing occasionally after sending a message.
  • Fixed crash in LoS tool when nodes are too spread out.
  • Removed accidental ability to chat with Room nodes.
  • Fixed Radio Button visual glitch on iPadOS 26.
  • Fix rooms saying "Connected" when the radio was manually disconnected

  • Fix T1000-E not matching to OCV preset

messages.author: avion
messages.text: Hi everyone, version 0.10.3 of MC1 was just submitted to TestFlight for review. This is mostly a bug fix release, but there are some nice quality of life changes too. It seems like the reviews are taking quite a long time now, so expect to wait up to a week for it to be approved. What's Changed Inserting reply quoted text is now off by default. You can re-enable it in Settings > Chats Added Packets Sent/Received to History graphs in Repeater Telemetry You can now search for nodes by typing their public key. This works in both the Nodes tab and in the Discover view. The Nodes list is now sorted by the radio's internal Last Modified time, instead of Last Advert. This should make it less confusing to see why a node hasn't been auto deleted. New Advanced Settings shortcut in the Radio Button menu Slightly adjusted chat message bubble padding and spacing Reduced memory usage in chat and conversation list views ## Bug Fixes Fixed a crash that could occur on app launch when the database failed to initialize Fixed chat messages remaining in input bar after sending Fixed trace path decoding to use the correct encoding format and hash mode Fixed several concurrency issues in chat message processing and conversation reloading Fixed Export Debug Logs button not working reliably Fixed selfInfo frame length validation (Thanks @robekl!) Fixed a bug that could cause excessive Bluetooth traffic after receiving messages @robekl Optimized RX Log pruning @robekl Thank you to those that have been sending such great feedback and bug reports! You're awesome.
messages.content_html:
Hi everyone, version 0.10.3 of MC1 was just submitted to TestFlight for review. This is mostly a bug fix release, but there are some nice quality of life changes too. It seems like the reviews are taking quite a long time now, so expect to wait up to a week for it to be approved.

What's Changed

  • Inserting reply quoted text is now off by default. You can re-enable it in Settings > Chats
  • Added Packets Sent/Received to History graphs in Repeater Telemetry
  • You can now search for nodes by typing their public key. This works in both the Nodes tab and in the Discover view.
  • The Nodes list is now sorted by the radio's internal Last Modified time, instead of Last Advert. This should make it less confusing to see why a node hasn't been auto deleted.
  • New Advanced Settings shortcut in the Radio Button menu
  • Slightly adjusted chat message bubble padding and spacing
  • Reduced memory usage in chat and conversation list views ## Bug Fixes
  • Fixed a crash that could occur on app launch when the database failed to initialize
  • Fixed chat messages remaining in input bar after sending
  • Fixed trace path decoding to use the correct encoding format and hash mode
  • Fixed several concurrency issues in chat message processing and conversation reloading
  • Fixed Export Debug Logs button not working reliably
  • Fixed selfInfo frame length validation (Thanks @robekl!)
  • Fixed a bug that could cause excessive Bluetooth traffic after receiving messages @robekl
  • Optimized RX Log pruning @robekl Thank you to those that have been sending such great feedback and bug reports! You're awesome.

messages.author: avion
messages.text: I really like the idea, but I'm not comfortable with adding it at the app layer since there's no easy way to change the path hash mode dynamically per channel. I believe the only way at the moment is to set the firmware setting which would lead to having to do the following for every message sent in a channel with a different path hash mode set: (Assume starting in 1 byte mode) Change path hash to 2 bytes (for example) Wait for .ok from firmware Send message Change path hash back to 1 byte Wait for .ok from firmware If this feature ever lands in the firmware, I'd be super happy to implement it in MC1. But until then, I don't think it would be wise to do everything at the app layer. But if there's something I'm misunderstanding about how the firmware works, I'm all ears. (edited)

messages.author: avion
messages.text: Did you happen to install the app via Xcode and then delete it? If you delete the app it removes the BLE device from iOS. And currently deleting the device causes all the data associated with it to be removed. So if you then opened the TestFlight app, it would see that the device was deleted from the system and cascade delete all data associated with it so it isn't orphaned. It makes developing a bit of a pain unless you have a separate device for testing though. I want to eventually create an app backup feature to export stored messages, discovered nodes, etc. because I do think this is a pain point.

messages.reply_html:
avion
Did you happen to install the app via Xcode and then delete it? If you delete the app it removes the BLE device from iOS. And currently deleting the device causes all the data associated with it to be removed. So if you then opened the TestFlight app, it would see that the device was deleted from the system and cascade delete all data associated with it so it isn't orphaned. It makes developing a bit of a pain unless you have a separate device for testing though. I want to eventually create an app backup feature to export stored messages, discovered nodes, etc. because I do think this is a pain point.

messages.author: avion
messages.text: Build 109 is now available This build introduces a new BLE throttler for ESP32 based devices to fix an issue where if the BLE link got saturated, MC1 would continue to flood it instead of backing off. This is likely what was causing some sync issues for ESP32 devices. I don't have an ESP32 device so please let me know if this helps (or makes things worse). What's Changed You can now fetch telemetry from companion type nodes. Minimally tested, please let me know any feedback you have on this! Even simple stuff like, "Why is there a blank row here?" Sharing a contact via advert button now has a green confirmation checkmark when it succeeds Added ability to change Direct Message Multi Acks in Advanced Settings Hide Channel 0 when fetching telemetry ## Bug Fixes Sharing a contact via advert when the node's advertisement data was missing or too old would show the wrong error message New BLE throttler for ESP32 based devices to prevent saturating the device's link Fixed a couple map coordinate bounding issues that caused crashes when loading nodes with invalid coordinates (edited)
messages.content_html:
Build 109 is now available

This build introduces a new BLE throttler for ESP32 based devices to fix an issue where if the BLE link got saturated, MC1 would continue to flood it instead of backing off. This is likely what was causing some sync issues for ESP32 devices. I don't have an ESP32 device so please let me know if this helps (or makes things worse).

What's Changed

  • You can now fetch telemetry from companion type nodes. Minimally tested, please let me know any feedback you have on this! Even simple stuff like, "Why is there a blank row here?"
  • Sharing a contact via advert button now has a green confirmation checkmark when it succeeds
  • Added ability to change Direct Message Multi Acks in Advanced Settings
  • Hide Channel 0 when fetching telemetry ## Bug Fixes
  • Sharing a contact via advert when the node's advertisement data was missing or too old would show the wrong error message
  • New BLE throttler for ESP32 based devices to prevent saturating the device's link
  • Fixed a couple map coordinate bounding issues that caused crashes when loading nodes with invalid coordinates
 (edited)

messages.author: avion
messages.text: I likely won't implement this because I don't want to encourage long messages in MeshCore to save airtime. As far as possible, I'm trying to make MC1 be respectful of airtime and other clients. I realize I'm being a bit of a hypocrite here because I implemented reactions which aren't part of the protocol (and I am somewhat regretting implementing it now because it does use double the airtime). But at the time, this was something I felt would add to the experience without causing too much annoyance to others. For MC1 users it improves the experience since they can now see MeshCore Open's reactions. It is still annoying to other client users as @gjelsoe mentioned above, but at least it is readable. As soon as MeshCore implements reactions at the protocol level, I'll adopt it in MC1 and get rid of the custom implementation. As much as possible, I don't want to create app-side hacks for features the protocol is missing. So I probably won't add a feature to auto split messages, even though I have wished for that using MC1 myself.

messages.content_html:
Build 113 is now available

This build contains syncing changes for both nrf52 and esp32 devices. Let me know if you notice any issues.

What's changed

  • You can now toggle Flood Routing when logging into a remote node
  • Scrolling through telemetry history charts is now much easier
  • Temperature, pressure, altitude, and distance now show in your locale's preferred units (Fahrenheit, inHg, feet for non-metric)
  • Voltage, current, and energy readings show three decimal places now (e.g. 3.742 V) to match chart precision
  • Deleting your device in System Bluetooth Settings no longer removes all associated data in MC1
  • "Forget Device" now offers two choices: unpair but keep your chat history and contacts, or unpair and delete everything

messages.text: Build 119 is now available The main feature in this release is Backup & Restore. This feature required a lot of background changes and data loss may occur. Please test and let me know if you experience any issues related to saved devices and/or data. What's Changed Backup/Restore feature. Back up all your app data including messages, notification settings, saved paths in the Trace Path Tool, etc. Import them into a completely separate iPhone/iPad if you'd like! Send DMs to channel users by long pressing on their message Room message URLs are now tappable "Discover Regions" now automatically adds repeaters to your device's contact list if it finds a repeater with a region Redesigned contact path editor. Now includes a search field and sort filters. Added OCV curves for T-Beam 1W and ThinkNode M6 ## Known Issue Filters in the "Add Hop" sheet are missing a background and are currently transparent
messages.content_html:
Build 119 is now available

The main feature in this release is Backup & Restore. This feature required a lot of background changes and data loss may occur. Please test and let me know if you experience any issues related to saved devices and/or data.

What's Changed

  • Backup/Restore feature. Back up all your app data including messages, notification settings, saved paths in the Trace Path Tool, etc. Import them into a completely separate iPhone/iPad if you'd like!
  • Send DMs to channel users by long pressing on their message
  • Room message URLs are now tappable
  • "Discover Regions" now automatically adds repeaters to your device's contact list if it finds a repeater with a region
  • Redesigned contact path editor. Now includes a search field and sort filters.
  • Added OCV curves for T-Beam 1W and ThinkNode M6 ## Known Issue
  • Filters in the "Add Hop" sheet are missing a background and are currently transparent
